svg.text: FULL CUDA-graph hooking — request timeline & async streams
vLLM-Hook v0.5.7 · buffer mode · cudagraph_mode = FULL_DECODE_ONLY (eager prefill + full-graph decode) · generic over QK / HS / steer workers
time →

g.text: QK: prefix-K read from paged KV
views; QK: append k_new / prefix-K; record ring evt
GPU clones → pinned host; record_stream guard;
overlaps next decode; ring event · bounds GPU
(default OFF → this stream does not exist)

svg.text: A · side.wait_stream(compute) — gather after scatter
B · fence_before_forward: compute.wait_event(prev egress) — cross-step overlap
C · fence_stream (drain waits egress)
D · drain_barrier — flush waits egress + drain events
Per-worker branches (the timeline above is shared; these are the differences)
QK · probe_hook_qk (capture on TP rank 0)
in-graph op: capture_qk — index_copy scatter of q + k rows
egress: index_select q + k; append k_new each step;
prefix-K read from paged KV off-graph (1st capture step)
flush: _k_all_cpu_list rebuilds each request's
growing-prefix K history from its own bucket
route key: _capture_idle_key (idle-skip only)
HS · probe_hidden_states (capture on TP rank 0)
in-graph op: capture_hs — forms hidden+residual,
then index_copy scatter
egress: index_select hs; NO prefix-K
flush: hidden_states stored directly —
NO reconstruction (residuals live in no cache)
route key: _capture_idle_key (idle-skip only)
Steer · steer_hook_act (runs on ALL TP ranks — residual is replicated)
in-graph op: steer_buffer — masked IN-PLACE residual add;
mutation propagates downstream on replay (mutates_args)
NO egress lane, NO drain, NO artifacts — the steered
residual just changes the emitted tokens
route key: (req_ids, qsl) signature → stable decode
skips build + upload entirely (pure replay)
Legend

g.text: Compute stream (GPU default) — forward + cudagraph replay + inline H2D routing upload
Egress copy stream — dedicated CUDA side stream, default ON (VLLM_HOOK_EGRESS_STREAM)
Drain D2H stream — dedicated CUDA side stream, opt-in / default OFF (bounds GPU residency)
Async save thread — host daemon thread, opt-in / default OFF (VLLM_HOOK_ASYNC_SAVE=1)
Driver / host Python — routing plan build, W1 idle-skip decision, flush & reconstruction
A side.wait_stream(compute) — egress gather ordered after this step's in-graph scatter
B fence_before_forward: compute.wait_event(prev egress) — next forward waits last step's gather
C fence_stream — drain waits the egress gather event before reading its output
D drain_barrier — flush blocks on egress + drain ring events only (not a full device sync)

svg.text: Load-bearing facts
• The in-graph op is NOT a splitting op → it is absorbed into the decode cudagraph and replays with 0 Python. All per-request Python lives outside the graph.
• The routing H2D upload is INLINE on the compute stream (a non_blocking pinned→device copy) — it is not a separate stream. Only egress + drain are true CUDA side streams.
• Static q/k/hs buffers are allocated at INSTALL before the cudagraph memory pool, so their data_ptr is fixed across replays and the recorded scatter always hits the right rows.
• Incremental routing (v0.5.7 A) uploads only columns whose active-layer-set changed; steady-state decode changes nothing → upload skipped, pure replay.
• Cross-step overlap: egress gather of step k runs on its side stream while the driver builds routing for step k+1; fence B stops k+1's forward from clobbering buffers still being read.
• With defaults (egress ON, drain OFF, async-save OFF) the only concurrency beyond the compute stream is the egress copy stream; enabling drain / async-save adds the two dashed lanes.
